skill.name: rn-testing
skill.description: Testing patterns for React Native with Jest, React Native Testing Library, Expo mocks, Zustand stores, React Query, hooks, and navigation flows.
skill.summary: Guide React Native test writing with Jest, Expo mocks, Zustand state resets, async waits, hooks, navigation, and API mocks.
skill.author: CJHarmath

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, credential, weak cryptography, network, and combined-danger patterns. Review found these are Markdown examples, test mocks, or substring matches inside normal testing terms, with no evidence of malicious intent or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: External Command Static Matches Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown code fences, inline code, and test-command examples. They do not execute commands when the skill is loaded. Lines 427-433 document npm test commands that a user may choose to run in a project.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is a Markdown guide, and the matched lines are fenced examples or inline code. No script file, runtime hook, or automatic command execution path is present in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Is A Local Test Mock
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The URL on line 140 is http://test-api.local inside a mocked expo-constants example. It is not used for real network communication or data transfer by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears inside a Jest mock configuration for Expo constants. The surrounding lines define static test data, not an active request.

low_findings.title: Credential And Weak Crypto Matches Are Substring False Positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The certificate or key finding maps to Object.keys in a test assertion. Weak cryptography findings map to words such as description, describe, and Design, not cryptographic APIs or algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines contain test framework vocabulary and JavaScript object inspection. I found no certificate material, private key, hashing code, cipher choice, or crypto library usage.

low_findings.title: Combined Dangerous Pattern Heuristic Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The critical heuristic combined the false positive command, local mock URL, and credential substring matches. No evidence found for code execution plus network exfiltration plus credential access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only scanned file is documentation and contains no runnable automation. The underlying findings driving the heuristic were each dismissed after reviewing context.

content.user_title: Test React Native Apps with Jest
content.value_statement: React Native tests often fail because native modules, async state, and store resets need precise setup. This skill gives concrete Jest and React Native Testing Library patterns for reliable app tests.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to reset Zustand stores between tests with setState.
Demonstrates async test patterns with act, waitFor, and findBy queries.
Provides Expo module mock examples for router, secure store, constants, and haptics.
Explains React Query test wrappers and disabled retry settings.
Covers component, hook, navigation, snapshot, and API hook testing patterns.
limitations: It is a Markdown guide and does not run tests automatically.
Examples assume Jest and React Native Testing Library are already installed.
Project import paths and generated API hook names must be adapted to each codebase.
It does not replace device testing or end-to-end testing for native behavior.

use_cases.title: Stabilize Store Tests
use_cases.description: Reset shared Zustand state before each test and verify async state changes without flaky bleed between cases.

use_cases.title: Mock Expo Dependencies
use_cases.description: Create consistent Jest mocks for Expo Router, Secure Store, Constants, and Haptics in unit tests.

use_cases.title: Debug Async Test Failures
use_cases.description: Apply act, waitFor, and findBy patterns to resolve warnings, timeouts, and missing UI assertions.

prompt_templates.title: Write A Basic Component Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Use rn-testing to write a React Native Testing Library test for this component. Include render assertions and one press interaction.

prompt_templates.title: Mock Expo Modules
prompt_templates.prompt: Use rn-testing to design Jest mocks for the Expo modules used by this screen. Explain where each mock should live.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Async Store Test Flakiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Use rn-testing to review this failing Zustand test. Add the correct store reset, act wrapper, and waitFor assertions.

prompt_templates.title: Plan A Full Test Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use rn-testing to propose a focused test plan for this React Native feature. Cover hooks, navigation, API hooks, loading states, and error states.

output_examples.input: A developer asks how to prevent Zustand test state from leaking between cases.
output_examples.output: The response recommends resetting the store before each test, reading fresh state after async actions, and avoiding assumptions about component re-renders.

output_examples.input: A test fails because Expo Router is unavailable in Jest.
output_examples.output: The response lists the router functions to mock, suggests placing the mock in global setup, and explains how to assert navigation calls.

output_examples.input: A component test shows an act warning after loading data.
output_examples.output: The response explains why the warning occurs and recommends waiting for the loaded UI state before finishing the test.

best_practices: Reset shared stores before every test that reads or writes global state.
Mock native modules at the same import path used by the component under test.
Assert user-visible outcomes and important state transitions instead of implementation details.
anti_patterns: Do not let async state updates continue after a test has already finished.
Do not snapshot large or frequently changing component trees.
Do not reuse one QueryClient across unrelated tests without clearing its cache.

faq.question: Does this skill run Jest tests for me?
faq.answer: No. It provides testing patterns and command examples, but the user or agent must run tests in the project.

faq.question: Which testing library does it target?
faq.answer: It targets Jest with React Native Testing Library for components, hooks, stores, and async UI behavior.

faq.question: Can it help with Expo projects?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes mock patterns for Expo Router, Secure Store, Constants, and Haptics.

faq.question: Does it cover Zustand stores?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains store reset patterns, fresh state reads, and async store action testing.

faq.question: Does it include React Query guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows a test QueryClient wrapper and settings that reduce retry-related test noise.

faq.question: Is it suitable for end-to-end testing?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on unit and integration-style tests, not full device automation.
